message:
test(conformance): audit live-oracle matrix coverage with exemption schema

Adds a conformance-level audit harness that verifies every
fr-conformance/fixtures/*.json file is either (a) registered in the
live_oracle_matrix.json suites array, (b) enumerated as an explicit
fixture exemption in the new live_oracle_audit_exemptions.json schema
with a human-readable reason, or (c) covered by the
user_workflow_corpus_v1.json differential_hook journey list. This
closes the drift loop where fixtures could land in the tree without
ever being wired into the live-oracle diff against upstream Redis.

- crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs grows three test-module helpers
  (`live_oracle_matrix_fixture_names`, `suite_fixture_files_on_disk`
  for the subset currently visible in the diff; the full helper set
  plus the audit assertion test bodies are added in the subsequent
  lines) and the paired audit test that compares the three sources
  and reports every unaudited fixture plus every dangling exemption
  entry with no matching on-disk fixture. Pulls in `BTreeMap`
  alongside the existing `BTreeSet` import since the exemption
  bookkeeping wants ordered-map semantics for deduplication.

- crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_audit_exemptions.json is
  the new schema_version=live_oracle_audit_exemptions/v1 registry with
  two top-level arrays:
    * fixture_exemptions (7 entries): core_blocking.json (multi-client
      wakeup orchestration not yet modelled), core_migrate.json
      (cross-instance MIGRATE needs a second Redis endpoint),
      core_pubsub.json (dedicated subscriber connections + streaming),
      core_wait.json (WAIT/WAITAOF AOF/replica matrix variations),
      fr_p2c_006_replication_handshake.json (handled by specialised
      handshake harness), persist_replay.json (replay differential
      path), smoke_case.json (local sanity check, not a diff suite).
    * planned_differential_hook_exemptions (1 entry): FR-P2C-009-J001
      (TLS/config live-oracle journey stays planned until the matrix
      can provision a TLS-enabled Redis + cert material).

Each exemption carries a `reason` field spelling out the structural
blocker, so future matrix uplifts can find the blocking work by
grepping for the exemption and remove it once the underlying
capability lands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
